text: **Anna:** Hi!
**Anna:** I see you {like|好き/好む|/laɪk/|I like pizza.} superhero {culture|文化/習慣|/ˈkʌltʃər/|Pop culture is interesting.}
**Anna:** Me too.
**Anna:** In fact, tonight I'm going to the big {superhero|スーパーヒーロー|/ˈsuːpərhɪəroʊ/|Superman is a famous superhero.} {convention|大会/集会|/kənˈvenʃən/|Comic convention is popular.}
**Young Man:** Are you going?
**Young Man:** Um
**Anna:** I don't know.
**Anna:** Well, you'd {better|よい/ましな|/ˈbetər/|You better hurry up.} decide soon.
**Anna:** Last year it {sold|売った|/soʊld/|He sold his car.} out.
**Anna:** So since we're talking about superheroes, would you rather {become|なる/変身する|/bɪˈkʌm/|I want to become a doctor.} a superhero?
**Anna:** By {accident|事故/偶然|/ˈæksɪdənt/|It was just an accident.} like Spider-Man, or be born a superhero like Wonder Woman.
**Anna:** Take your time.
**Anna:** It's a big question.
**Anna:** I {thought|考えた|/θɔːt/|I thought about it carefully.} about it for days.
**Young Man:** Okay, if I had to choose, I'd rather be born a superhero.
**Anna:** I'd rather become a superhero
**Anna:** By an {unexpected|予期せぬ/突然の|/ˌʌnɪkˈspektɪd/|An unexpected gift arrived.} accident.
**Young Man:** Aren't all accidents unexpected?
**Anna:** Well, yeah.
**Young Man:** What was that?
**Young Man:** Are you okay?
**Anna:** I'm
**Anna:** Better than okay.
**Anna:** I feel {super|超/非常に|/ˈsuːpər/|That's super cool.}
**Anna:** Oh no, Anna was just hit by {lightning|稲妻/雷|/ˈlaɪtnɪŋ/|Lightning struck the tree.}
**Anna:** She had better get help.
**Anna:** We use had better to give advice.
**Anna:** It is very {informal|くだけた/非公式の|/ɪnˈfɔːrməl/|Informal language is casual.} and stronger than should.
**Anna:** Ought to.
**Anna:** For example, Anna says, you'd better decide soon.
**Anna:** Last year it {sold|売れた/売り切れた} out.
**Anna:** When we use
**Anna:** {Had better|〜した方がよい} we usually {shorten|短縮する} the word had for personal pronouns.
**Anna:** We use {would rather|〜したい} to say what we or someone else prefers to do or have.
**Anna:** For example, the boy says, Okay, if I had to choose, I'd rather be born a {superhero|superhero/スーパーヒーロー}.
**Anna:** With would rather, we also shorten the word would when used with personal pronouns.
**Anna:** Keep watching and listen for
**Anna:** Or had better and would rather.
**Anna:** You'd better see a {doctor|医者}.
**Anna:** I've never felt better.
**Anna:** You were just {struck|打たれた/襲われた} by lightning and what happened to your hair and your clothes?
**Anna:** I don't know.
**Anna:** Wait, I do know.
**Anna:** This is my {super suit|スーパースーツ} and this is my {origin story|起源の物語}.
**Young Man:** What are you talking about?
**Anna:** An {origin story|起源の物語} tells the beginning of a superhero.
**Anna:** You should know that.
**Anna:** You're not making any sense, lady.
**Anna:** I would rather be called
**Announcer:** Lightning boat lady!
**Anna:** It'll sound great in a {theme song|テーマソング}!
**Announcer:** Lightning boat lady!
**Anna:** Now I need to find my {superpowers|超能力}!
**Young Man:** Um, I really think wait, don't tell me
**Anna:** I'll read your mind.
**Anna:** You are thinking you'd like to be my {super helper|スーパーヘルパー}.
**Young Man:** I was not thinking that.
**Anna:** That you'd like to live in a {tree house|ツリーハウス}.
**Young Man:** No.
**Anna:** That you should eat more {vegetables|野菜}.
**Young Man:** Please {stop|止める|/stɒp/|Stop talking right now.} talking.
**Young Man:** You really should get some {help|助け/支援|/help/|Can you help me, please?}.
**Anna:** Mine reading is not
**Anna:** my superpower.
**Anna:** Maybe I can become {invisible|見えない|/ɪnˈvɪzəbəl/|Ghosts are invisible.}.
**Anna:** Hmm, I am invisible.
**Anna:** You can't see me.
**Anna:** Who am I?
**Anna:** I'm not here.
**Anna:** You can't see me.
**Young Man:** I can see you and so can everybody else.
**Anna:** No power of {invisibility|不可視性|/ɪnvɪzəˈbɪləti/|Invisibility is a superhero power.}.
**Anna:** Maybe I can create a {force|力/強制|/fɔːrs/|Gravity is a natural force.} field
**Anna:** I feel it working.
**Anna:** Nothing can hurt.
**Anna:** Ow, that wasn't very {nice|良い/親切な|/naɪs/|That was a nice gift.}.
**Anna:** I see I have a lot of work to do.
**Anna:** Well goodbye, {non-super|並の/普通の|/nɒn-ˈsuːpər/|A non-super hero is just normal.} person.
**Anna:** Wait, I'd better go with you, you might get {worse|悪化する|/wɜːrs/|His condition got worse.}.
**Anna:** That's even possible.
**Anna:** That's very nice of you, ordinary human
**Anna:** But I'd rather go by myself.
**Anna:** This is a {quest|探求/冒険|/kwest/|A quest for treasure is exciting.}.
**Young Man:** Every time you speak I get more {confused|混乱した|/kənˈfjuːzd/|I'm confused by this problem.}.
**Anna:** A quest is a part of all superhero stories.
**Anna:** You really need to work on your superhero stuff.
**Anna:** Now stand back.
**Anna:** I've never {flown|飛んだ|/floʊn/|Birds have flown over the mountain.} before.
**Young Man:** And you're not
**Young Man:** Flying now.
**Anna:** {Flying|飛ぶこと/空を飛ぶ|/ˈflaɪɪŋ/|Birds are flying high.} is not my {superpower|超能力|/ˈsuːpərpaʊər/|Superhero movies show superpowers.}.
**Anna:** That's too bad.
**Anna:** It's going to be {expensive|高価な/費用がかかる|/ɪkˈspensɪv/|Luxury cars are expensive.} to Uber everywhere.
**Anna:** You know, I'd rather {walk|歩く|/wɔːk/|Let's walk to the park.}.
**Anna:** It's a nice day.
**Anna:** Goodbye, {non-superperson|超能力のない人|/nɒn-ˈsuːpərpɜːrsən/|A normal person without special abilities.}.
**Young Man:** I'm not talking to {strangers|見知らぬ人|/ˈstreɪndʒərz/|Don't talk to strangers.} again.
**Announcer:** Will {Lightning|稲妻/電光|/ˈlaɪtnɪŋ/|Lightning strikes during storms.} Boat Lady find her {superpowers|超能力|/ˈsuːpərpaʊərz/|Superheroes have amazing superpowers.} ever?
**Announcer:** Ouch!
**Anna:** Did that {brick|レンガ|/brɪk/|The wall is made of brick.} wall hurt?
**Anna:** Will this young man ever talk to a stranger again?
**Announcer:** Find out on the next episode of Let's Learn English!
